named_technique: Bombieri-Lang conjecture (effective) + Faltings-Vojta height inequality on Jacobian of the cyclotomic-quotient curve
seminal_paper_arxiv: Faltings 'Diophantine approximation on abelian varieties' Annals of Math 133 (1991) 549-576; Vojta 'Diophantine Approximations and Value Distribution Theory' Lecture Notes in Math 1239 (1987); Bombieri-Lang conjecture survey Hindry-Silverman 'Diophantine Geometry' GTM 201 (2000) Part F.
bridge_lemma: Treat (p, k) solutions of Phi_q(p) = k * p^(q-1) as integral points on the cyclotomic-quotient curve C_q : y = Phi_q(x) with Jacobian J(C_q) an abelian variety of dimension g(C_q) = (q-3)(q-4)/2 (28, 45, 91, 120 for q in {11,13,17,19} by Riemann-Hurwitz). Bombieri-Lang plus Faltings 1991 yield finiteness of integral points on the genus >= 2 curve and Vojta 1987 supplies a non-uniform per-q height bound H_q. Closure however comes not from H_q but from the elementary residue Phi_q(p)/p^(q-1) < p/(p-1) at most 3/2 hence under 2 for odd p >= 3, forcing integer k = 1 against k >= 2.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (single-prime collapse). q prime + sigma_0(n) = q forces n = p^(q-1) for an odd prime p. Step 2 (curve realization). Treat varying (p, k) as integral points on the affine cyclotomic-quotient curve C_q : y = Phi_q(x) over Q. The smooth projective model has geometric genus g(C_q) = (q-3)(q-4)/2 by Riemann-Hurwitz on the degree-(q-1) Phi_q cover ramified at the (q-1) roots of unity plus infinity. For q in {11, 13, 17, 19} this gives g = 28, 45, 91, 120. Step 3 (Jacobian embedding). The Abel-Jacobi map embeds C_q into its Jacobian J(C_q), an abelian variety of dimension g >= 28. Step 4 (general-type subvariety). Once embedded, C_q is a curve of geometric genus >= 2 on the abelian variety J(C_q), so by Faltings 1991 (effective Mordell on Jacobians, the classical case of Bombieri-Lang) the set C_q(Q) is finite; integral points (p, k) over Z inherit finiteness. Step 5 (Vojta height inequality). Vojta 1987 provides h(P) << epsilon * deg(P) + C(V, epsilon) for P outside an exceptional locus; specialised to C_q in J(C_q), this gives h(p, k) <= H_q for a constant H_q depending on q (canonical class, embedding, genus). H_q is NOT uniform in q: it grows with K_{C_q} which grows linearly in g(C_q) ~ q^2/2. Step 6 (height bound is scaffold, not closure). Even with effective H_q, the bound alone does not rule out k >= 2. The closure comes from the elementary residue. Step 7 (elementary residue closure). For odd prime p >= 3 and q >= 2, sigma(p^(q-1))/p^(q-1) = (p^q - 1)/((p-1) p^(q-1)) = 1 + 1/p + ... + 1/p^(q-1) < 1 + 1/(p-1) = p/(p-1) <= 3/2 < 2. Hence the integer k must equal 1, contradicting k >= 2. Step 8 (uniform-in-q dispatch). Finset.forall_mem of {11,13,17,19} dispatches via the q-parametrised template. Step 9 (analog map). Faltings-Vojta's role is structural: it gives the abelian-variety / general-type framing justifying a priori finiteness. This is qualitatively distinct from Wave-1 (Mihailescu cyclotomic-unit residue), Wave-2-Subspace (ESS rank bound), and Wave-2-Chabauty (NEGATIVE rank bound on Jacobian). The honest contribution is the structural framing PLUS elementary closure; the height bound is conjectural-effective and not load-bearing. Step 10 (Lean portability). Even though Faltings/Vojta/Bombieri-Lang are not in Mathlib, the closure step formalizes via the elementary k < 2 bound, with Faltings-Vojta abstracted as a `have : Finite { (p, k) : ... }` stub. The conditional theorem ('IF Bombieri-Lang closes finiteness, THEN multiperfect impossibility') is honestly formalizable; the actual closure does not need the conjectural scaffold.
candidate_lemmas: card_divisors_prime_collapse: For q prime and n > 1 with Odd n, (Nat.divisors n).card = q iff there exists odd prime p with n = p^(q-1). Mathlib: Nat.card_divisors, Nat.Prime.prime, Nat.divisors_prime_pow.
sigma_prime_pow_geom: For p prime and e >= 0, (Nat.divisors (p^e)).sum id = (p^(e+1) - 1)/(p - 1) = 1 + p + p^2 + ... + p^e. Mathlib: Nat.divisors_prime_pow, Finset.geom_sum_eq.

geom_series_ratio_lt_two: For p >= 3 prime and e >= 1, (1 + p + p^2 + ... + p^e) < 2 * p^e. Equivalent to (p^(e+1) - 1)/(p - 1) < 2 * p^e, which holds because (p^(e+1) - 1)/(p - 1) = p^e * (p - p^(-e))/(p-1) < p^e * p/(p-1) <= p^e * 3/2 < 2 * p^e. Elementary, no Faltings or Bombieri-Lang machinery.

k_must_equal_one: If p >= 3 prime, e >= 1, and k * p^e = (p^(e+1) - 1)/(p-1) with k positive integer, then k = 1. Combines sigma_prime_pow_geom and geom_series_ratio_lt_two; this is the load-bearing closure step.

genus_cyclotomic_quotient_curve: For q prime, the smooth projective model of C_q : y = Phi_q(x) has geometric genus g(C_q) = (q-3)(q-4)/2 by Riemann-Hurwitz applied to the degree-(q-1) Phi_q cover ramified at the (q-1) roots of unity plus infinity. Mathlib: no direct genus API; would require AlgebraicGeometry.RiemannRoch infrastructure (not present).

abelian_variety_jacobian_embed: For C_q a smooth projective curve of genus g >= 2 over a number field K, the Abel-Jacobi map embeds C_q into its Jacobian J(C_q), an abelian variety of dimension g over K. Mathlib: AbelianVariety class does not exist; AlgebraicGeometry.Curve.Jacobian is a stub.

faltings_mordell_curve_general_type: For C an irreducible smooth projective curve of geometric genus >= 2 over a number field K, the set of K-rational points C(K) is finite. (Faltings 1983 / 1991 Annals.) Mathlib: NOT formalized (100-theorems wishlist).

vojta_height_inequality_stub: For A an abelian variety, V a subvariety of general type, and theta an ample line bundle on A, for every epsilon > 0 there exists C(V, theta, epsilon) such that for all P in V(K-bar) outside an exceptional Zariski-closed proper subvariety, h_theta(P) < epsilon * deg_theta(P) + C(V, theta, epsilon). (Vojta 1987 Lecture Notes 1239.) Mathlib: NOT formalized.

bombieri_lang_finiteness_stub: For A an abelian variety over a number field K, V a subvariety of A of general type, the set of K-rational points V(K) is contained in a finite union of proper closed subvarieties of V; in particular if V is a curve of geometric genus >= 2 then V(K) is finite. (Bombieri-Lang conjecture; proven for curves = Faltings.) Mathlib: NOT formalized.

conditional_closure_under_bombieri_lang_then_dispatch: IF the set { (p, k) : Z x Z | p prime, p >= 3, k >= 2, k * p^(q-1) = (p^q - 1)/(p-1) } is finite for each q in {11, 13, 17, 19} (Faltings-Vojta-Bombieri-Lang) and the elementary residue k < 2 holds, then the set is empty; Finset.forall_mem dispatches the uniform-in-q theorem, with uniformity inherited from the elementary bound, not from Faltings-Vojta which is q-dependent.
honest_disclaimer: This is YOLO-W3-A, angle D (Wave 3). Distinct from: Wave-1 (slot 1293, Mihailescu primary-cyclotomic-unit residue Phi_q(p) ≡ 1 mod p); Wave-2 Subspace (slot 1299, Evertse-Schlickewei-Schmidt rank-uniform Subspace Theorem, fit_score 0.78); Wave-2 Chabauty (slot 1297, NEGATIVE Chabauty-Coleman feasibility witness, fit_score 0.18). Three LLM consultations dispatched May 30 2026: (a) Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh-reasoning (extensive Mathlib search confirmed Faltings/Vojta/Bombieri-Lang absent from Mathlib); (b) Grok-4-fast-reasoning via xAI API with web_search + x_search tools (confirmed no effective Bombieri-Lang in literature, derived Riemann-Hurwitz genus, identified the elementary k < 2 closure as load-bearing); (c) Gemini-2 (quota exhausted at submission time, fallback used). fit_score = 0.42 reflects (i) the Bombieri-Lang / Faltings-Vojta scaffold is genuinely structural but NOT load-bearing: the closure comes from the elementary k < 2 bound, not the height inequality, (ii) the height bound is NON-UNIFORM in q (Vojta constants depend on canonical class which grows with genus), so the framing fails the strict 'uniform-in-q' criterion, (iii) Faltings / Vojta / Bombieri-Lang are absent from Mathlib, so any formal contribution must abstract them as axiomatic stubs OR fall back on the elementary closure, (iv) the elementary closure (k < 2) is identical to what wave-2 Subspace ultimately reduced to, so wave-3 contributes a different STRUCTURAL FRAMING with the SAME elementary residue. The honest path is: state the bare gap, attach this fusion dossier explaining the Bombieri-Lang framing, and let Aristotle decide whether to (A) take the abelian-variety / general-type framing as a meaningful structural lift, (B) collapse to the elementary closure (which it already produced for q = 11 at slot 746), or (C) reject the framing as overkill. paired_llm_consultations: 3 (codex-exec, grok-api-search, gemini-fallback). The cross-domain transplant: where Faltings 1991 used height inequalities on abelian varieties to prove Mordell, and Vojta 1987 generalised this to higher-dimensional subvarieties of general type, we transplant the abelian-variety / general-type framing to the cyclotomic-quotient curve C_q whose Jacobian J(C_q) is the natural abelian-variety embedding of the multiperfect equation. The honest sub-claim closure (CS = sub_claim_closure) is the uniform-in-q multiperfect impossibility for {11,13,17,19} via elementary residue closure; the Bombieri-Lang framing is the STRUCTURAL SCAFFOLD, not the load-bearing step. Risk of bounded-version overclaim is mitigated by the explicit Finset {11,13,17,19} target and the sub_claim_closure declaration. real_closure_candidate = false (the Bombieri-Lang scaffold is conjectural-effective, the elementary closure overlaps with wave-2). Plausibility of full closure for the q-family: ~0.72 (high because the elementary bound is easy, same as wave-2). Plausibility of a meaningfully Bombieri-Lang-flavoured sub-claim getting formalized: ~0.15 (Faltings-Vojta not in Mathlib).
